resolution_note: The dye anchor of the pilot. Dye now survives mainly in desktop photo printers; large-format has shifted to pigment.
Desktop unit rated per-print, not m2/hr. Bordered A3+ colour ~90 sec.

original_value: 11 colours + Chroma Optimizer (12 channels: MBK PBK C M Y Gy PC PM PGy R B + CO)

resolution_note: 90 sqft/hr (~8.4 m2/h) per dealer guide; Canon rates an A1 in ~32s. Fine-art speed, not production.
HP moved DesignJet to pigment for durability - dealer notes pigment prints last longer than dye for wall displays.

original_value: up to 120 A1/D pages per hour (21 sec/A1)
resolution_note: CAD/technical machines rated in A1 or D pages/hour, not m2/hr - different metric than the graphics machines.
Line accuracy is a CAD-specific spec that photo/graphics machines don't quote - reflects the technical-drawing application.

original_value: 6-colour: C M Y PK MK GY
Glossy photo: Speed 361, Quality 195, Max Quality 89 sqft/hr; Plain: Draft 1571, Speed 1200 sqft/hr
resolution_note: 361 sqft/hr photo speed mode; up to 1571 sqft/hr plain-paper draft. Highly mode-dependent - both preserved.
The only non-scanning machine in the pilot. A stationary printhead spans the full page width; media moves under it in one pass - fundamentally different from the scanning carriage of the other four.

original_value: up to 20 D/A1 pages per minute; Pro up to ~400 m2/hr
resolution_note: 20 D/A1 pages/min (single-pass); the Pro variant is quoted up to ~400 m2/hr. Vastly faster than the scanning machines - the point of pagewide.

original_value: up to 4 media rolls with auto-switching
